# Contributing

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-04 -->

Thanks for helping improve `contentful-management.py`.

## Prerequisites

| Tool | Version | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Python | 3.9–3.12 | See `pyproject.toml` → `requires-python = ">=3.8"` (CI matrix: 3.9–3.12) |
| PDM | Latest | `pip install pdm` — used for venv, deps, and publishing |
| Docker | Any recent | Required for the dev container workflow |
| Dev Container CLI | Latest | `npm install -g @devcontainers/cli` — optional, for terminal-based devcontainer |

## Getting Started

The recommended path is the **dev container** — it pins the Python version and installs all dependencies automatically.

### Option A: Dev Container (recommended)

**VS Code:**
Open the repository in VS Code, install the Dev Containers extension if needed, then run `Dev Containers: Reopen in Container`. The container runs `pdm install --group test` on creation.

**Terminal:**
```bash
# Install dev container CLI
npm install -g @devcontainers/cli

# Start container and enter it
devcontainer up --workspace-folder .
devcontainer exec --workspace-folder . bash
```

### Option B: Local Python environment

```bash
# Clone
git clone git@github.com:contentful/contentful-management.py.git
cd contentful-management.py

# Install dependencies (test group required to run tests)
pdm install --group test    # source: .devcontainer/devcontainer.json → postCreateCommand

# Verify
pdm run coverage            # source: pyproject.toml → tool.pdm.scripts.coverage
```

## Development Workflow

All commands below are run via `pdm run <script>` — scripts are defined in `pyproject.toml` → `[tool.pdm.scripts]`.

```bash
# Run tests only (unittest discover)
pdm run test            # source: pyproject.toml → scripts.test

# Run linter only
pdm run lint            # source: pyproject.toml → scripts.lint (runs tox -e lint)

# Run full coverage (lint + coverage report)
pdm run coverage        # source: pyproject.toml → scripts.coverage

# Run full tox matrix (all Python versions)
pdm run test-all        # source: pyproject.toml → scripts.test-all

# Clean all build/test artifacts
pdm run clean           # source: pyproject.toml → scripts.clean
```

## Testing

- **Framework:** `unittest` (standard library `unittest.discover`)
- **HTTP mocking:** VCR cassettes via `vcrpy` — HTTP interactions are recorded as YAML fixtures in `fixtures/` and replayed offline. Tests never make live API calls.
- **Location:** `tests/` — one `*_test.py` file per source module
- **Coverage report:** `pdm run coverage` runs `coverage run --source=contentful_management -m unittest discover` then `coverage report -m` — source: `tox.ini` `[testenv:coverage]`
- **Tox matrix:** Python 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 — source: `tox.ini` `env_list`

**Adding tests for a new resource:** Mirror the existing pattern — create `tests/<resource>_test.py` and `tests/<resource>_proxy_test.py`. Add VCR fixtures to `fixtures/<resource>/`.

**Do not make live API calls in tests.** VCR cassettes ensure deterministic, offline test runs.

## Code Style & Conventions

- **Linter:** `flake8` — configured in `tox.ini` `[flake8]`; ignores `E501` (line length), `E231`, `E203`, `F401`, `F403`, `E261`, `E122`, `F811`
- **Module docstrings:** Standard triple-quoted docstrings at module level (existing pattern throughout codebase)
- **snake_case → camelCase conversion:** The `utils.snake_case` and `utils.camel_case` helpers translate between Python naming and the CMA JSON API
- **`sys` block access:** Always access resource metadata through the `self.sys` dict, not the raw `item` dict

## Commit Convention

Recent commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):

```
type(scope): description [TICKET-ID]
```

Valid types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `docs`, `refactor`, `test`, `ci`, `build`

Examples:
```
feat: add taxonomy concept endpoints
fix: preserve fields default value on update
chore: route CI alerts to sdk-bots channel
docs: add agent section to README
build(deps): update requests dependencies [DX-886]
```

## Branch Strategy

- `master` — default branch
- Feature/fix branches — create from `master`, e.g., `feat/taxonomy-endpoints`, `fix/serialization-bug`

## Pull Requests

1. Fork and create a branch from `master`
2. Run checks inside the dev container: `pdm run coverage`
3. Open a PR with a short summary of the change
4. CI runs the full test matrix across Python 3.9–3.12 using the devcontainer

## CI/CD

| Job | Trigger | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `Test (Python 3.9)` | Push/PR to `master` | Runs `pdm run coverage` + `pdm run lint` inside devcontainer |
| `Test (Python 3.10)` | Push/PR to `master` | Same |
| `Test (Python 3.11)` | Push/PR to `master` | Same |
| `Test (Python 3.12)` | Push/PR to `master` | Same — default Python version in devcontainer |

Source: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
